title: baseline.repo.json — the repo descriptor (C39)
description: The repo's declared identity and posture: the one stored piece of intent every applicability, severity, and join derivation hangs off. Deliberately minimal and schema-bound — that is what stops it regrowing into a status blob. Keys prefixed with _ are inline notes and are ignored by the validator. Consumed by src/descriptor.mjs; every field's consumer (active or reserved) is asserted by --self-check.

schema_version.description: Descriptor format version. Lets the engine refuse or migrate a descriptor written for a different contract.

type.description: What this repo is. Declared, not sniffed — supersedes filesystem auto-detection so a tooling package.json can't misclassify a docs repo as node.

maturity.description: Discrete maturity tier. Gates scrutiny that rises with maturity — e.g. the CLAIM category activates at 'claimed'. x-strictness (ascending) is DESC-03's weakening ladder: a down-move is a posture weakening (M6).

workflow.description: Agent-workflow posture. multi-lane is the V2 default (any solo dev gets lanes); single-lane is the V1-equivalent readiness-only posture; multi-lane-local (CF5) is the reduced git-plane mode — claim and leases work from origin alone, the forge is never consulted, and forge-dependent rules SKIP with the posture named. x-strictness (ascending) is DESC-03's weakening ladder (M6).

anchoring.description: Anchoring strictness knob (C15). Orthogonal to workflow — a single-lane repo may still anchor. x-strictness (ascending) is DESC-03's weakening ladder (M6).

ground_truth_boundary.description: Which ground-truth planes are authoritative and reachable (C29). Consumed by the capability probe and the target-ref policy.

default_branch.description: The target ref enforcing contexts read the descriptor from (FS1). Bounded like the lane globs: the value rides into git argv (origin/<branch>).

lanes.description: Multi-lane workflow configuration. namespace is consumed by `lane claim` (M5a); lease_ttl by the lease derivation (M5b).

namespace.description: Branch-name pattern for lane claims, e.g. lane/*. Exactly one '*', which the claimed issue number replaces — the ref IS the claim (FS2), so the pattern must yield one deterministic name per issue.

lease_ttl.description: Lease horizon (nonzero); a lane with no activity within it derives as ABANDONED and is reclaimable. Freshness = max(tip committedDate, PR updatedAt) — FS10 as amended.

families.description: The repo's OTHER declared branch families beyond the lane namespace (globs, e.g. adopt/*, release/*) — the real branch inventory. FLOW-04 checks branch placement against namespace + families, so an undeclared family surfaces as a finding instead of wallpapering every legitimate branch. Bounded (each ≤64 chars; ≤64 entries) since the globs ride into a matcher.

join_keys.description: Declared join keys (trailers/frontmatter fields) the relational join is allowed to use. An unresolvable join is a finding, never an inference.

engine_pin.description: Engine version the repo is wired for. Skew-detection input.

staleness.description: Freshness ceilings for derived, bounded-stale views (orient).

facts_ttl.description: How long a cached forge fact is served before re-fetch.

orient_ceiling.description: Hard age ceiling past which orient labels its view stale.
